timeline.cause: The rollout is pulling an image tag that doesn't exist (registry.internal/payments:v2.4.11): the registry answers 'manifest not found', so new pods can never start. Old replicas are still serving — this is a bad deploy, not an outage yet.

timeline.out:
deployment.apps/payments
REVISION  CHANGE-CAUSE
11        deploy registry.internal/payments:v2.4.10 (last good)
12        deploy registry.internal/payments:v2.4.11 (current, ImagePullBackOff)
timeline.note: identify the last known-good revision before choosing a rollback target

timeline.explain: Roll back explicitly to revision 11, the history table's last-good image. Zero-downtime: the old pods are still up.
timeline.rollback: kubectl -n prod rollout undo deploy/payments --to-revision=11  # explicit last-good target from rollout history
timeline.followup: Fix the tag in the pipeline (looks like a typo) and re-deploy.

timeline.text: All replicas Running and Ready — restarts flat. Standing down.

title: payments rollout stuck on a tag that doesn't exist
